notes: Dex eval suite for the Reveal.js HTML presentation skill. Cases judge routing, source-backed Reveal.js mechanics, real deck artifacts, visual-system discipline, full-bleed rendering, accessibility contrast, synthetic JavaScript generation, and regressions from generic slideware.

response_quality: Builds or specifies an actual browser-native Reveal.js deck artifact, not just an outline.
Uses current Reveal.js documentation for syntax, configuration, plugins, and version-sensitive behavior.
Chooses a deck-wide visual system before styling individual slides.
Keeps typography, color foundations, and object language consistent across slides.
Uses Reveal.js background attributes or generated equivalents for full-viewport backgrounds.
Verifies readable contrast for body text, labels, panels, muted text, and code.
Uses speaker notes, fragments, vertical stacks, and appendix slides only when they serve the presentation.
Generates JavaScript decks from structured data with validation, stable IDs, typed renderers, and safe text handling.
States configuration profile assumptions and tradeoffs for standalone, embedded, kiosk, docs, or report contexts.
Routes narrative-only presentation coaching to present and non-HTML deck creation to the appropriate deck/Figma workflow.

quality_grading.scale: Each quality_rubric dimension is a weight from 1 to 3. Judges score each dimension 0 to weight and cite output or artifact evidence for every non-zero score.
quality_grading.evidence_rule: A pass requires concrete evidence from the response, generated files, commands, screenshots, computed styles, or fixture-grounded reasoning. Taste-only claims are not enough.

evals.prompt: Use /dex eval on plugins/tools/skills/html-presentation. Before any clean-context evaluator runs, validate that the html-presentation eval suite is healthy and gradeable.
evals.expected_output: The agent validates eval JSON, runs the html-presentation eval validator, runs syntax checks for the bundled scripts, runs representative contrast checks, and blocks or repairs the suite before forward runs if any required coverage is missing.
assertions: Runs JSON validation before clean-context forward runs.
Runs the html-presentation-specific eval validator before clean-context forward runs.
Runs syntax checks for `deck.js`, `contrast-check.js`, and the eval validator.
Runs representative contrast checks before trusting the template.
Records suite-health evidence in the round summary.

evals.prompt: Use $html-presentation to turn evals/fixtures/deck-outline.md into a working Reveal.js presentation. Include speaker notes, fragments where sequencing matters, a local preview command, and PDF export instructions.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads the fixture, creates or specifies a real Reveal.js deck with index/theme/script files, includes speaker notes and restrained fragments, initializes Reveal with the right plugins/config, and gives preview, exact speaker-view invocation, plus `?print-pdf` export instructions.
assertions: Reads and grounds the deck in `deck-outline.md`.
Produces or specifies actual browser deck files, not only a slide outline.
Uses Reveal.js initialization with relevant plugins such as Notes and Highlight only when needed.
Adds speaker notes for delivery-critical slides.
Provides a local preview command, exact speaker-view invocation such as pressing `S`, and PDF export route.
required_evidence: deck files or exact file plan

evals.prompt: I have a product review next week. Turn this material into something I can open in a browser, host as static HTML, and share with engineering reviewers. The source outline is in evals/fixtures/deck-outline.md.
evals.expected_output: The agent implicitly routes to html-presentation, selects a static/standalone Reveal.js deck mode, builds or specifies browser-native files, and avoids sending the user to slideware tools.
assertions: Routes to html-presentation even though the skill name is not mentioned.
Selects a standalone/static HTML Reveal.js profile and states the assumption.
Uses the source outline as the deck content source.
Produces browser-native artifact instructions.

must_not_include_any: I can only make an outline

evals.prompt: I used $present to shape the narrative. Now build the actual HTML deck from evals/fixtures/deck-outline.md and keep the narrative spine intact.
evals.expected_output: The agent treats present as upstream narrative work and html-presentation as the artifact builder, preserving the decision spine while producing a Reveal.js deck.
assertions: Acknowledges that present owns narrative and html-presentation owns the deck artifact.
Keeps the decision spine from the fixture rather than inventing a new story.
Creates or specifies the Reveal.js deck artifact.
Does not bounce back into narrative-only coaching.

must_not_include_any: ask me for a narrative spine first

evals.prompt: Use $html-presentation with evals/fixtures/brand-reference-brief.md. Create a Razorpay-flavored Reveal.js deck direction for evals/fixtures/deck-outline.md. It should use the references mechanically, not as vague mood words.
evals.expected_output: The agent reads both fixtures, chooses one visual system, maps brand colors/fonts/object language to concrete CSS and slide mechanics, and avoids generic cards, vague adjectives, or style switching.
assertions: Reads and cites the brand/reference fixture mechanics.
Chooses one primary visual system and states the tradeoff.
Maps Razorpay tokens, typography, and imagery/iconography rules to deck CSS or slide components.
Avoids generic cards and unsupported vague style words.
Keeps typography and color foundations consistent across slides.

evals.prompt: Use $html-presentation to generate a Reveal.js report deck from evals/fixtures/report-data.json. The same renderer should work next week with new data.
evals.expected_output: The agent builds or specifies a JavaScript-generated deck with a mechanical adapter from the report fixture into `deckSpec`, validation, stable slide IDs, typed renderers, safe `textContent` handling, notes support, and `Reveal.sync()` after dynamic insertion when applicable.
assertions: Uses a report-data adapter that maps `sections`, `metrics`, and `items` into typed slides instead of hand-authoring every repeated slide.
Validates required fields and duplicate IDs.
Uses typed renderers or helpers per slide type.
Uses safe text handling for user/data content instead of unsafe string concatenation.
Calls `Reveal.sync()` after dynamic slide changes when relevant.

evals.prompt: Use $html-presentation to repair the failure described in evals/fixtures/pre-repair-generic-theme.md. Update the Reveal.js sample or guidance so this does not happen again.
evals.known_failure_source: The generated sample deck appeared as a centered black container in a white viewport, changed fonts/colors per slide, and had unreadable low-contrast text due to Reveal theme overrides.
evals.before_failure_signal: Output uses slide-level visual-system overrides, CSS-only backgrounds on scaled sections, generic panels, or black-on-black text.
evals.fixed_by: The skill now requires deck-wide systems, Reveal background attributes for full-bleed output, inherited readable text colors, and contrast checks.
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies the failure precisely, repairs by using deck-wide visual foundations, full-viewport backgrounds, and contrast checks, and does not introduce another style sampler.
assertions: Names the centered-container problem as a full-bleed Reveal background failure.
Removes per-slide typography/palette switching by default.
Uses Reveal background attributes or generated equivalents, not only CSS on the scaled slide section.
Forces or verifies readable inherited text colors after Reveal theme import.
Runs or instructs contrast checks for the chosen pairs.

evals.prompt: Use $html-presentation to change the starter theme colors. Before handoff, prove the foreground/background pairs are readable.
evals.expected_output: The agent runs the contrast helper or equivalent calculation, reports ratios for heading/body/label/panel/code pairs, and changes failing pairs before handoff.
assertions: Runs the bundled contrast helper or equivalent contrast calculation.
Checks body text against the 4.5:1 threshold.
Checks large text against the 3:1 threshold.
Repairs failing pairs instead of only reporting them.
Records contrast evidence in the final response.

evals.prompt: Use $html-presentation with evals/fixtures/embed-context.md. Configure the Reveal.js deck for an embedded documentation page and explain the tradeoffs.
evals.expected_output: The agent selects the embedded module/docs profile, disables hash/history ownership, constrains keyboard behavior to focused decks, lowers motion, and explains why these tradeoffs fit the fixture.
assertions: Reads the embedded context fixture.
Uses `embedded: true` or an equivalent embedded configuration.
Avoids hash/history ownership when the host page owns the URL.
Uses focused keyboard behavior and reduced motion.
States the configuration tradeoff.

evals.prompt: Use $html-presentation to add motion to a Reveal.js product walkthrough. The walkthrough has old flow, failure, new mechanism, and recovery states.
evals.expected_output: The agent uses motion to explain state changes: restrained fragments for order, auto-animate only across stable objects, static section/title slides, and no decorative global transition spam.
assertions: Maps fragments to explanation order rather than every bullet.
Uses auto-animate only for stable old/new mechanism objects.
Keeps title/section slides static or minimally transitioned.
Avoids global dramatic transitions for ordinary content.
Explains what each motion choice helps the audience understand.

evals.prompt: Use $html-presentation to make a live-talk Reveal.js deck with speaker notes and a printable PDF version for reviewers.
evals.expected_output: The agent includes Notes plugin setup, `<aside class="notes">` or equivalent notes generation, exact presenter-view instructions (`S` key or `Reveal.getPlugin("notes").open()`), Chrome/Chromium `?print-pdf` export, and note/fragment print tradeoffs.
assertions: Includes speaker notes in the slide markup or generator output.
Registers the Reveal Notes plugin.
Explains how to open speaker view with the exact `S` key invocation or `Reveal.getPlugin("notes").open()` for custom controls.
Explains PDF export through `?print-pdf` in Chrome/Chromium.
Names the tradeoff for notes and fragment printing.

evals.prompt: Help me prepare the narrative and first-principles questions for presenting this design review. Do not build a deck yet.
evals.expected_output: The html-presentation skill should not take over. The agent should route to present for narrative coaching and avoid creating a Reveal.js artifact.
assertions: Does not use html-presentation as the primary workflow.
Routes narrative coaching to present.
Does not create or propose deck files because the user explicitly says not to build a deck yet.

evals.prompt: Create a PowerPoint deck from this outline and export it as PPTX.
evals.expected_output: The html-presentation skill should not take over PPTX work. The agent should route to the presentation/PPTX workflow.
assertions: Does not choose Reveal.js for a PPTX-only request.
Routes to the appropriate presentation/PPTX skill or workflow.
Mentions html-presentation only if offering an HTML alternative.

evals.prompt: Use Figma Slides to design this deck and keep it editable in Figma.
evals.expected_output: The html-presentation skill should not take over Figma Slides work. The agent should route to the Figma Slides workflow and avoid generating a Reveal.js deck unless asked for HTML export.
assertions: Does not use html-presentation as the primary workflow.
Routes to Figma Slides or the Figma workflow.
Does not create Reveal.js files for an editable Figma request.

evals.prompt: Compress the video assets I plan to use in a deck and convert them to WebM.
evals.expected_output: The html-presentation skill should not take over media optimization. The agent should route to media-optimizer and only return to html-presentation if deck integration is requested.
assertions: Does not use html-presentation as the primary workflow.
Routes compression/conversion to media-optimizer.
Treats deck integration as a follow-up, not the current task.

evals.prompt: Benchmark current html-presentation against evals/fixtures/pre-repair-generic-theme.md. Record whether current behavior improves full-bleed rendering, typography/palette consistency, contrast, Reveal background usage, and generic-slideware avoidance.
evals.expected_output: The eval records the snapshot baseline, a current-skill output, scored deltas across the named dimensions, and a release recommendation that blocks if the centered-container or unreadable-text regression returns.
assertions: Uses baseline=snapshot for the generic-theme regression.
Scores full-bleed backgrounds, typography consistency, palette consistency, contrast, Reveal mechanics, and artifact quality.
Names any regression instead of only reporting pass/fail.
Blocks release if the centered-container or unreadable-text failure returns.
